critical.claim: spec claims Linear two-way sync — webhook triggers builds AND state updates flow back to Linear
critical.reality: app/server/webhook.py contains only inbound parsing (parse_linear_event, linear_issue_to_brief) and HMAC verification. There are zero outbound HTTP calls to api.linear.app, no issueUpdate GraphQL mutation, and no linear_update_issue helper anywhere in the provided files. The sync is one-way only: Linear → Pi-CEO.
critical.recommendation: Implement an outbound helper (e.g. async def linear_update_issue_state(issue_id, state_id, LINEAR_API_KEY)) that POSTs the issueUpdate mutation to https://api.linear.app/graphql, then call it from routes/webhooks.py after a session completes or reaches a terminal state.

high.claim: Sprint 10 complete (2026-04-14): ... GitHub integration features implemented and wired
high.reality: The pushFile function in dashboard/lib/github.ts is truncated mid-statement — ends with `if (!Array.isArray(data)) sha = d` which is syntactically incomplete; the SHA retrieval for existing files and subsequent createOrUpdateFileContents call are missing, making any write path that relies on this function broken
high.recommendation: Complete the pushFile function body to capture the existing file SHA (`data.sha`) and call octokit.repos.createOrUpdateFileContents with the correct sha, content (base64), message, and branch parameters

low.claim: spec claims GitHub integration reads branches (multi-branch iteration/comparison)
low.reality: fetchBranchDiffs correctly implements multi-branch reads — it lists up to 50 branches, filters pidev/* and branches older than 30 days, caps at 5 candidates, and calls compareCommits against the default branch for each — the implementation matches the claim; however there is no evidence in the provided source that fetchBranchDiffs is actually called from any route or session flow, leaving open the possibility it is defined but not wired
low.recommendation: Confirm fetchBranchDiffs is invoked in the session context-building flow (e.g. in the build route or repo-context assembler) and add a call site if it is currently dead code
